encounters.name: Medical Emergency
encounters.description: Your comms crackle with a distress call: a small freighter reports a crew member suffering acute radiation exposure. They're requesting any medical supplies you can spare.

choices.label: Dock and Treat the Exposure
choices.description: Burn time and supplies stabilizing their crew. Radiation waits for nobody.

outcome.description: You transmit emergency treatment protocols and dock briefly to transfer supplies. The freighter captain thanks you profusely and insists on paying for the help.

choices.label: Log It for the Next Patrol
choices.description: Flag the coordinates and keep flying. Someone closer might reach them in time.

outcome.description: You log the distress coordinates for the next patrol and continue on your route. The signal fades behind you.

encounters.name: Engine Failure
encounters.description: A cargo hauler is adrift with a blown fuel converter. The pilot sounds panicked  -- their orbit is decaying toward an asteroid field. They're offering credits for a tow to the nearest station.

choices.label: Tow Them
choices.description: Burn extra fuel to tow the hauler to safety.

outcome.description: The tow takes time and fuel, but the pilot's relief is palpable. They transfer payment the moment you reach the station.

choices.label: Boost Their Beacon
choices.description: Amplify the signal for local patrols. Faster than towing, but no guarantee anyone's listening.

outcome.description: You amplify their signal to reach local patrols. It's the best you can do without losing time. Hopefully help arrives in time.

encounters.name: Navigation Error
encounters.description: A civilian transport has drifted off course after a nav computer malfunction. Their passengers are getting anxious, and the pilot is too proud to admit they're lost.

choices.label: Share Nav Data
choices.description: Transmit your navigation charts to get them back on course.

outcome.description: The pilot accepts your data with minimal embarrassment. A grateful passenger sends you a small tip through the ship's comm channel.

choices.label: Let Them Sort It Out
choices.description: The lanes are charted. A pilot who can't read a star map has bigger problems than you can fix.

outcome.description: You leave them to sort out their own heading. Space is big, but the lanes are well-charted. They'll manage.

encounters.name: Abandoned Freighter
encounters.description: Your sensors detect a derelict freighter drifting in the void. No life signs, no active transponder. The cargo bay appears partially intact  -- there could be salvageable goods aboard.

choices.label: Crack the Cargo Bay
choices.description: Dock with the wreck and see what the previous owner left behind. Dead ships don't file insurance claims.

outcome.description: You pick through the wreckage carefully. Most of the cargo is ruined, but you find a sealed crate of trade goods and some spare parts worth selling.

choices.label: Scan from a Safe Distance
choices.description: Map the wreck without setting foot inside. Salvage companies pay for survey data too.

outcome.description: Your sensors map the wreck's layout and log the debris pattern. The data might be useful to salvage companies. You tag the location for later.

choices.label: Give It a Wide Berth
choices.description: Dead ships mean dead crews. Whatever happened here, you don't need to know.

outcome.description: You give the derelict a wide berth and continue on your route. Someone else can deal with it.

encounters.name: Battle-Scarred Wreck
encounters.description: A ship bearing heavy weapons damage floats dead ahead. Scorch marks suggest a recent firefight. The wreck could hold valuable components  -- but whatever hit this ship might still be nearby.

choices.label: Risk Salvage
choices.description: Board quickly and grab what you can.

outcome.description: You work fast, stripping the ship of its remaining weapons components and emergency fuel reserves. No sign of whoever did this  -- today is your lucky day.

choices.label: Steer Clear
choices.description: Fresh battle damage means trouble nearby.

outcome.description: Discretion wins out. You alter course and put distance between yourself and whatever happened here. The wreck shrinks on your rear sensors.

encounters.name: Traveling Merchant
encounters.description: A small trading vessel hails you with a friendly ping. The captain identifies herself as an independent trader running surplus goods between systems. She offers a discount if you're buying.

choices.label: See What She's Got
choices.description: Surplus runs between systems usually mean below-market prices. Worth a look.

outcome.description: After a quick negotiation, you pick up some goods at well below market rate. The trader waves as she undocks and continues on her route.

choices.label: Wave Her Off
choices.description: Your hold is full or your schedule is tight. Either way, not today.

outcome.description: You exchange polite farewells and go your separate ways. Space is lonely enough without being rude about it.

encounters.name: Supply Convoy
encounters.description: A Guild-flagged supply convoy is making a scheduled fuel run. The convoy leader offers to top off your reserves at cost  -- professional courtesy between spacers.

choices.label: Top Off the Tanks
choices.description: Fuel at cost is fuel at cost. You don't turn down that kind of courtesy.

outcome.description: The convoy docks briefly and transfers fuel to your tanks. The crew chief tips his cap as they disengage. Good people still fly these lanes.

choices.label: Swap Route Intel Instead
choices.description: Credits come and go. Good navigation data pays dividends for weeks.

outcome.description: You swap navigation data and market tips. The convoy leader marks a few profitable routes on your charts. Knowledge is currency out here.

encounters.name: Scattered Cargo
encounters.description: Your proximity sensors light up  -- a cargo container has broken apart, scattering crates across your flight path. Looks like someone lost a shipment. Finders keepers?

choices.label: Scoop What You Can
choices.description: Somebody's loss is your margin. The crates won't collect themselves.

outcome.description: You maneuver through the debris field, snagging intact crates with your cargo grapple. A few are damaged, but most contain sellable goods.

choices.label: Plot Around the Debris
choices.description: Loose crates mean a rough breakup. Whatever scattered this cargo might still be nearby.

outcome.description: You plot a course around the floating junk and continue on your way. Not every opportunity is worth the risk.

encounters.name: Sensor Ghost
encounters.description: Your sensors register a brief anomalous reading  -- mass signature, thermal spike, then nothing. Could be debris, could be a sensor glitch, could be something more interesting.

choices.label: Chase the Reading
choices.description: Burn twenty minutes tracking a phantom. Could be nothing. Could be the interesting kind of nothing.

outcome.description: You spend time chasing the signal. Turns out it was a chunk of ice sublimating near a heat vent. Not exciting, but you learn to calibrate your sensors better.

choices.label: File It Under 'Unexplained'
choices.description: Your sensor log is full of ghosts. One more won't change anything.

outcome.description: You log the anomaly and move on. Your nav computer files it under 'unexplained readings'  -- a list that grows longer every week.

encounters.name: Energy Phenomenon
encounters.description: An unusual energy signature pulses ahead  -- a swirling pattern of charged particles that doesn't match any known natural formation. Collective research buoys mark the area as 'under study.'

choices.label: Hold Position and Record
choices.description: Park at the edge and let your instruments drink it in. The Collective pays well for exotic data.

outcome.description: Your instruments record exotic particle interactions that push the boundaries of known physics. The Collective would pay well for this data.

choices.label: Leave It to the Specialists
choices.description: The research buoys are there for a reason. Log the coordinates and let someone with proper shielding handle it.

outcome.description: You log the coordinates and give the phenomenon a respectful berth. Some things are better left to the specialists.

encounters.name: Pirate Toll
encounters.description: A scarred gunship drops out of hiding and locks weapons on your hull. The pilot's voice is bored but firm: "Standard toll for passing through. {shakedown_demand} credits, and you fly on. Refuse, and we do this the hard way."

choices.label: Pay the Toll
choices.description: Hand over the credits and avoid a fight.

outcome.description: You transfer the credits. The pirate grunts acknowledgment and peels away. Cheaper than repairs, you tell yourself.

choices.label: Power Up Weapons
choices.description: The toll is your dignity. Today you're not paying.

outcome.description: You power up weapons and angle your shields. The pirate grins  -- they were hoping you'd say that.

encounters.name: Sector Enforcer
encounters.description: A heavily armed patrol vessel intercepts you. The captain claims this sector is under their protection and demands a {shakedown_demand} credit "security fee." Their weapons are hot.

choices.label: Pay Fee
choices.description: Pay the fee and move along.

outcome.description: The enforcer accepts payment without ceremony. They mark your transponder as 'cleared'  -- whatever that means out here.

choices.label: Stand Your Ground
choices.description: Call their bluff.

outcome.description: You hold course and charge weapons. The enforcer's professional demeanor cracks  -- they expected compliance, not resistance.

choices.label: Claim Authority
choices.description: Insist you have official clearance.

outcome.description: You transmit a confident-sounding string of authorization codes. The enforcer hesitates, then backs off  -- they can't risk tangling with someone who might have real backing.

encounters.name: Distress Signal  -- Disabled Convoy Escort
encounters.description: Your comms light up with a repeating distress signal. A Guild convoy escort is adrift  -- engines disabled, life support failing. The captain is broadcasting on an open channel: 'This is Captain Sato of the Guild escort Heron. We were hit by raiders. Engines are gone. Life support has maybe six hours. If anyone can hear this...'

choices.label: Answer the Signal
choices.description: Divert from your route to assist the disabled escort. It will cost time and fuel, but someone's life is at stake.

outcome.description: You pull alongside the Heron and patch your fuel line into their auxiliary systems. Captain Sato's voice comes through, steady but exhausted: 'I owe you one, Captain. The raiders took everything  -- cargo, navigation data, even the crew manifests. This wasn't random. They knew exactly where we'd be.' She transfers a small credit bounty from the escort's emergency fund.

choices.label: Press On
choices.description: You have cargo to deliver and a schedule to keep. Someone else will answer the signal.

outcome.description: You silence the distress channel and continue on course. The signal fades behind you. Somewhere out there, Captain Sato is counting the hours on her life support.

encounters.name: Routine ID Check
encounters.description: A Guild patrol cruiser slides into formation alongside you, running lights blinking in the standard hail pattern. The officer's voice is clipped and professional: 'Unidentified vessel, transmit registration and manifest for routine inspection.'

choices.label: Hand Over Your Papers
choices.description: Clean manifest, clean conscience. Let the bureaucracy do its thing.

outcome.description: The officer scans your documents and nods to herself. 'Everything checks out. Safe travels, Captain.' The cruiser peels off and resumes its patrol route. A clean record has its perks.

choices.label: Comply  -- and Ask About the Lanes
choices.description: A patrol officer who's chatty is worth more than ten nav updates. Buy goodwill with small talk.

outcome.description: The officer warms up once your papers clear. She mentions increased raider activity two systems over and flags a few safe waypoints on your chart. 'Watch yourself out there,' she says before signing off.

encounters.name: Encrypted Fragment
encounters.description: Your comms array picks up a burst of encrypted traffic on a non-standard frequency. The signal is fragmented  -- repeating coordinates, a callsign you don't recognize, and what sounds like a countdown.

choices.label: Attempt Decryption
choices.description: Run the signal through your ship's cipher banks.

outcome.description: Your systems crack the outer layer of encryption. It's a supply drop schedule  -- someone is running unauthorized cargo through this sector. The coordinates alone are worth something to the right buyer.

choices.label: Dump the Raw Data to Archive
choices.description: Log the frequency and move on. Maybe it's useful later. Maybe it's just another ghost on a dead channel.

outcome.description: You dump the raw signal data to your ship's archive. Maybe you'll find a use for it later. Maybe it's nothing. The Expanse is full of ghosts on dead frequencies.

encounters.name: Overcrowded Transport
encounters.description: A battered civilian transport limps past, hull patches visible even at range. Dozens of faces crowd the viewports. A weary voice crackles over comms: 'We left Port Adira three days ago. No food, water's low. Can anyone spare supplies?'

choices.label: Open Your Stores
choices.description: You have more than they do. It will cost you reserves, but those faces in the viewport are hard to forget.

outcome.description: You dock and hand over what you can spare. The transport captain grips your hand through the airlock. 'We won't forget this.' A few passengers pool their remaining credits as thanks.

choices.label: Point Them to the Nearest Aid Depot
choices.description: You can't feed sixty people, but you can give them a heading. Sometimes hope is enough fuel.

outcome.description: You mark the nearest aid depot on their charts and boost their comms signal. It's not much, but it gives them a heading and a hope. The captain's voice breaks as she thanks you.

choices.label: Close the Channel
choices.description: Sixty refugees, one ship. The math doesn't work. It never does.

outcome.description: You close the channel and adjust course. The transport dwindles on your aft sensors. Out here, hard choices are the only kind.

encounters.name: Rare Goods Collector
encounters.description: A sleek private yacht hails you, its hull gleaming with custom plating. The owner introduces himself as a collector of curiosities from the outer systems. He's offering top credit for anything unusual in your hold.

choices.label: Open the Manifest
choices.description: Let the rich man paw through your hold. His idea of 'junk' and yours might differ profitably.

outcome.description: The collector's eyes light up at a few items you'd considered junk. He pays handsomely, insisting they have 'tremendous provenance.' You pocket the credits and wonder if you've been undervaluing your cargo.

choices.label: Drive a Hard Bargain
choices.description: Play up the rarity of your goods.

outcome.description: You spin a tale about the provenance of every item in your hold. The collector sees through most of it but respects the hustle. He pays a fair price and tips his hat as he departs.

choices.label: Not Selling Today
choices.description: Collectors pay well, but you know what your cargo is worth. Maybe more than he does.

outcome.description: The collector shrugs with practiced grace. 'Another time, perhaps.' His yacht glides away, its engines humming with the quiet confidence of old money.

encounters.name: Dormant Minefield
encounters.description: Your proximity alarm screams as your sensors paint a constellation of small metallic objects ahead. Old mines  -- relics of a border skirmish decades past. Most are dormant, but a few still show faint power signatures.

choices.label: Thread the Gaps at Quarter Thrust
choices.description: Slow and steady through a field of dormant ordnance. The map data alone is worth the sweat.

outcome.description: You creep through at quarter thrust, sensors on full sweep. It takes the better part of an hour, but you emerge on the other side with hull intact and a detailed minefield map that salvage teams would pay for.

choices.label: Burn Fuel on a Detour
choices.description: An hour of extra travel beats a mine through the hull. Your insurance doesn't cover relics.

outcome.description: You plot a wide arc around the minefield. It costs you time, but your hull plating thanks you. Some shortcuts aren't worth taking.

encounters.name: Failing Life Support
encounters.description: A faint distress beacon pulses from a small prospector's vessel. The pilot's voice is thin, words measured to conserve air: 'CO2 scrubbers failed. I've got... maybe two hours. Please.'

choices.label: Hard-Dock and Cycle Your Atmosphere
choices.description: Two hours of air from your reserves could mean the difference. You can smell recycled air for a while.

outcome.description: You hard-dock and cycle fresh atmosphere into the prospector's cabin. Color returns to her face as she gulps clean air. She insists on transferring her claim credits  -- 'Won't need them if I'm dead.'

choices.label: Pump Power Into Their Beacon
choices.description: Light up every receiver in the sector. Faster than docking, but someone else has to be close enough to care.

outcome.description: You pump power into their beacon until it lights up every receiver in the sector. A mining barge responds within minutes. You move on, hoping two hours was a conservative estimate.

encounters.name: Abandoned Relay Station
encounters.description: A pre-Expansion relay station hangs in the void, its solar arrays dark and twisted. The structure predates modern navigation  -- built when these lanes were first charted. Faint power readings flicker from deep inside.

choices.label: Suit Up and Go Inside
choices.description: Pre-Expansion stations have century-old databanks. Historians pay well for primary sources.

outcome.description: The station's corridors are silent and cold. You find the main control room still partially functional  -- its databanks contain century-old star charts and communication logs. Historians would pay well for this.

choices.label: Scan Without Docking
choices.description: Map the architecture from your cockpit. Less risk, less reward, but old stations have old problems.

outcome.description: Your scan reveals the station's architecture in detail  -- pre-Expansion construction techniques, obsolete but fascinating. You compile the data into a survey package worth a modest sum.

choices.label: Keep Flying
choices.description: It's been dark for a century. Whatever's inside can wait another century.

outcome.description: You log the coordinates and continue on. The station's dark silhouette shrinks behind you, keeping its secrets for the next curious traveler.

encounters.name: Gravity Anomaly
encounters.description: Your ship lurches as an invisible force tugs at the hull. Instruments spin wildly  -- a localized gravity distortion, dense and shifting, like something massive that isn't there. The void around it shimmers with lensed starlight.

choices.label: Ride the Gradient
choices.description: Hold at the edge and let your instruments record. Twenty minutes of harrowing readings the Collective would kill for.

outcome.description: You ride the gravity gradient for twenty harrowing minutes, instruments drinking in data. The readings are extraordinary  -- spatial compression at a scale that shouldn't exist this far from a stellar body. The Collective will want this.

choices.label: Slam the Throttle
choices.description: Your hull is groaning. Whatever this is, it's stronger than your ship. Get out while you still can.

outcome.description: You slam the throttle and fight free of the distortion's grip. Your hull groans but holds. Behind you, the lensing effect pulses once and fades. You don't look back.

encounters.name: Backchanel Deal
encounters.description: A nondescript freighter matches your velocity, running dark  -- no transponder, no hail frequency, just a tight-beam text burst: 'Got surplus. Good prices. No manifests. Interested?'

choices.label: Dock Dark and Browse
choices.description: No transponders, no manifests. The prices are good because the provenance is bad.

outcome.description: You dock in silence  -- no names, no pleasantries. The goods are quality, probably lifted from a Guild shipment. You pay a fraction of market rate and part ways with a nod. What the manifest doesn't show won't hurt it.

choices.label: Note Their Position
choices.description: Log their coordinates for the next patrol you meet.

outcome.description: You memorize their heading and vessel profile. A Guild patrol would pay for this kind of tip. The smuggler vanishes into the dark, oblivious.

choices.label: Decline Silently
choices.description: Not worth the risk.

outcome.description: You send a single character reply: 'N.' The freighter kills its tight-beam and drifts away without a sound. Clean conscience, empty pockets.

encounters.name: Void-Drifter Swarm
encounters.description: Your viewport fills with drifting shapes  -- bioluminescent organisms the size of cargo crates, trailing tendrils of soft blue light. Void-drifters, feeding on the ionized particles that stream between stars. They pulse gently against your hull, curious and harmless.

choices.label: Cut Engines and Film
choices.description: Drift among them with cameras rolling. Xenobiologists pay a fortune for behavioral footage.

outcome.description: You drift among them for half an hour, cameras rolling. The organisms seem to communicate through light patterns  -- complex, almost rhythmic. Your footage will make some biologist's career.

choices.label: Snag a Juvenile
choices.description: Research stations pay for live specimens. The little ones fit in a cargo grapple.

outcome.description: You extend the cargo grapple and snag a juvenile drifter. It glows indignantly in the specimen container, its tendrils tapping at the glass. Research stations pay well for live samples.

choices.label: Coast Through on Momentum
choices.description: Kill the engines and drift. For a few minutes, the void feels almost welcoming.

outcome.description: You cut engines and coast through the swarm on momentum alone. The drifters part around your hull like a luminous curtain. For a few minutes, the void feels almost welcoming.

encounters.name: Barter Trader
encounters.description: A weathered hauler pulls alongside, its captain leaning into the comm with a crooked grin. 'Credits are tight, friend, but I've got cargo to move. You interested in a straight swap? Goods for goods, no banks involved.'

choices.label: Haggle Over the Airlock
choices.description: Goods for goods, no banks, no fees. The old way of doing business out here.

outcome.description: After twenty minutes of good-natured haggling, you swap cargo holds. You're not sure who got the better deal, but the hauler captain seems pleased. He seals it with a handshake through the airlock.

choices.label: Stick With Credits
choices.description: Barter is a gamble. You'd rather know exactly what you're getting.

outcome.description: 'Fair enough,' the captain shrugs. 'Can't blame a man for trying.' He tips an imaginary hat and fires up his engines. The hauler rumbles away, leaving a faint trail of exhaust.

encounters.name: Ship Under Attack
encounters.description: Weapons fire flashes in the distance  -- a freighter is taking hits from two raider skiffs, its shields flickering. The captain's voice cuts through the static: 'Mayday! Any vessel  -- we're under attack! They're cutting through our --' The transmission dissolves into noise.

choices.label: Burn Hard Toward the Fight
choices.description: Two against two changes the odds. The raiders might break off when they see you coming.

outcome.description: You burn hard toward the firefight, weapons hot. The raiders see you coming and break off  -- two against two isn't the odds they wanted. The freighter captain nearly weeps with relief and transfers every credit she can spare.

choices.label: Light Up Every Patrol Channel
choices.description: Broadcast the coordinates and pray someone's in range. Safer than fighting, but slower.

outcome.description: You light up every patrol channel in range with the freighter's coordinates. A Guild corvette responds within minutes, but by the time it arrives, the raiders have stripped the freighter and fled. At least the crew survived.

choices.label: Alter Course
choices.description: Two raider skiffs against a freighter. Adding your ship to the body count doesn't help anyone.

outcome.description: You alter course and give the battle a wide berth. The weapons fire fades from your sensors. You tell yourself there was nothing you could have done. It almost sounds convincing.

encounters.name: Lone Escape Pod
encounters.description: A single escape pod tumbles end over end through the void, its emergency beacon pulsing weakly. No parent vessel in sight. The pod's power cells are nearly drained  -- whoever is inside doesn't have long.

choices.label: Grapple It and Crack the Seal
choices.description: Whoever's inside has been tumbling for days. The power cells are almost dead  -- this is their last window.

outcome.description: You snag the tumbling pod and crack it open. Inside, a shivering engineer blinks in the sudden light. She was aboard a mining vessel that broke apart three days ago. She transfers her emergency fund and asks for a ride to the nearest station.

choices.label: Lock a Power Umbilical On
choices.description: Match the tumble, charge the batteries, boost the beacon. Let a rescue crew do the rest.

outcome.description: You match the pod's tumble and lock a power umbilical onto its hull. The batteries drink in the charge, and the beacon flares back to full strength. A rescue vessel should pick it up within hours.

encounters.name: Old Earth Broadcast
encounters.description: Your comms array catches a signal on a frequency nobody uses anymore  -- an old Earth music broadcast, centuries out of date, bouncing between relay stations that should have gone dark long ago. A piano sonata fills your cockpit, hauntingly clear.

choices.label: Trace the Signal
choices.description: Follow the relay chain to find the source.

outcome.description: You trace the signal through three dead relays to a fourth that shouldn't exist  -- a tiny automated station, jury-rigged to keep broadcasting. Someone went to great trouble to keep this music alive. You archive the broadcast and log the station's coordinates.

choices.label: Just Listen
choices.description: Enjoy the music while it lasts.

outcome.description: You lean back and let the sonata wash over you. For a few minutes, the vastness outside your viewport feels less empty. The signal fades as you drift out of range, taking the music with it.

encounters.name: Danger Advisory
encounters.description: A patrol frigate cuts across your bow, its warning lights cycling amber. The officer broadcasts on an open channel: 'Attention all vessels  -- we've confirmed raider activity in the next sector. Multiple contacts, well-armed. Proceed at your own risk.'

choices.label: Ask for Tactical Data
choices.description: Raider positions, ship profiles, ambush points. This kind of intel saves lives.

outcome.description: The officer shares tactical data  -- raider positions, ship profiles, their preferred ambush points. You update your nav charts with safe corridors. This kind of intel saves lives.

choices.label: Volunteer for the Sweep
choices.description: One more ship on a raider flush changes the math. Risky, but the bounty makes it worth considering.

outcome.description: The officer raises an eyebrow but doesn't refuse. You join a short-range sweep that flushes out a raider scout. The patrol commander logs your contribution and transfers a bounty payment.

choices.label: Log It and Tighten Your Route
choices.description: Forewarned is forearmed. Adjust your nav charts and keep your shields warm.

outcome.description: You log the advisory and tighten your route planning. Forewarned is forearmed. The patrol frigate resumes its sweep as you push deeper into the lane.

encounters.name: Cold Signal
encounters.description: Your comms pick up a distress beacon  -- automated, looping, no voice. The signal metadata is eleven days old. The coordinates point to empty space. No ship on sensors.

choices.label: Divert to the Beacon Source
choices.description: Eleven days is a long time. Whatever you find won't be a rescue. But someone should look.

outcome.description: You find debris. Not much  -- a sheared hull panel, frozen coolant, a cargo manifest floating in the void. The ship's name was the Perihelion. Crew of four. Nobody's coming for them anymore. You log the coordinates and move on.

choices.label: Flag It in the Sector Registry
choices.description: Somewhere an office will update a file. Somewhere else, a family is still waiting. That's not your fault.

outcome.description: You flag the beacon in the sector registry. Somewhere, an office will update a file. Somewhere else, a family is still waiting for a ship that isn't coming back.

encounters.name: Witness Request
encounters.description: A weak signal, barely cutting through static: 'This is the freighter Sundog. We're not asking for rescue. We're asking for a witness. Someone needs to see what happened to us.' The voice is calm. Too calm.

choices.label: Bear Witness
choices.description: Approach the signal and document what you find.

outcome.description: The Sundog is venting atmosphere from a dozen hull breaches. Not pirates  -- the damage pattern is internal. Stress fractures in the alloy composite. Cheap materials, corners cut. The captain's last log names the manufacturer and the contract that approved the hull grade. You copy everything.

choices.label: Move On
choices.description: You don't owe strangers your time. Or your nightmares.

outcome.description: You cut the channel and adjust course. The signal fades. Whatever happened to the Sundog, it'll happen to someone else eventually. That's how corners get cut.

encounters.name: Already Handled
encounters.description: A distress signal flares on comms  -- a mining shuttle with engine trouble. You're plotting an intercept course when a second signature appears: a cargo hauler, already docking with the shuttle. Someone beat you to it.

choices.label: Open a Channel to the Hauler
choices.description: They beat you to it, but an extra pair of hands never hurts. At minimum, you get a name to remember.

outcome.description: 'Appreciate it, but we've got it handled,' the hauler captain replies. 'Name's Maren. We run this lane twice a week  -- you see enough breakdowns, you keep a tow rig handy.' You're not the only one out here who stops. Good to remember.

choices.label: Resume Your Route
choices.description: Someone else's good deed. The lane doesn't need two heroes today.

outcome.description: You watch the hauler guide the shuttle toward the nearest station and resume your route. One less problem in the lane. Someone else's good deed.

encounters.name: The Sleeping Dog
encounters.description: Sensors flag a small derelict  -- a single-crew courier, engines cold, transponder dead. The hull is intact. No signs of weapon damage. The cockpit lights are still on.

choices.label: Dock and Open the Cockpit
choices.description: The lights are still on. Intact hull, no weapons damage. Something happened to the pilot. You should find out what.

outcome.description: The pilot is still in the chair. Natural causes, the med-scanner says  -- heart failure. The nav computer shows a course for Haven's Rest. The cargo hold has a single crate: children's clothes, hand-sewn, carefully folded. A note on top reads: 'For Emi. See you Thursday.' You seal the ship and transmit the location to the nearest station. Someone should know.

choices.label: Scan and Transmit a Notice
choices.description: Confirm the situation from your cockpit and notify the nearest station. Let someone else open that door.

outcome.description: Scans confirm one deceased occupant. No hazards. You log the coordinates and transmit a salvage notice to the nearest station. The cockpit lights will keep burning until the battery dies. Then the void takes another one.

choices.label: Give It a Wide Berth
choices.description: Every pilot knows the numbers. Most of them choose not to think about it.

outcome.description: You give it a wide berth and keep moving. Every pilot knows the numbers. Most of them choose not to think about it.

encounters.name: The Manifest
encounters.description: A gutted freighter hull drifts in a slow tumble. The cargo bay has been stripped clean  -- salvage crews already picked the bones. But the nav console is still powered, flight recorder blinking.

choices.label: Pull the Nav Logs
choices.description: The flight recorder tells a story. Data like this has value  -- or consequences. Depends who reads it.

outcome.description: The manifest tells a story: medical supplies, purchased legally at Axiom Labs, bound for Breakstone. The last entry is a course correction  -- toward Crimson Reach. Someone rerouted the shipment. The medical supplies never reached the miners. The data might interest someone. Or it might get you in trouble.

choices.label: Strip the Console for Parts
choices.description: A functional power cell and some wiring. The story in the logs is none of your business.

outcome.description: You pull the flight recorder, some wiring, and a functional power cell. The story in the nav logs is none of your business. Most stories out here aren't.

choices.label: Mark It and Keep Flying
choices.description: The lane is full of bones. You can't stop for all of them.

outcome.description: You mark it on your chart and keep flying. The lane is full of bones. You can't stop for all of them.

encounters.name: Ghost Signal
encounters.description: Your comms array catches a transmission fragment  -- a ship-to-ship hail, automated, bouncing off relay buoys. The call sign doesn't match any registered vessel. The transmission timestamp is from six months ago, still echoing through the network.

choices.label: Follow the Relay Chain
choices.description: Seven buoys, a route that doesn't exist on current charts. Someone was using dead infrastructure. Find out who.

outcome.description: The signal bounced through seven relay buoys  -- a route that doesn't exist on current charts. Someone was using decommissioned infrastructure. The origin coordinates point to deep space between Crimson Reach and The Fulcrum. There's nothing there. Officially.

choices.label: Flush the Buffer
choices.description: Six-month-old ghost on a dead frequency. The Expanse is full of echoes. Not all of them mean something.

outcome.description: You flush the comm buffer and the ghost disappears. Relay networks are old, poorly maintained, full of echoes. Not everything that beeps means something. That's what you tell yourself.

encounters.name: The Graveyard Shift
encounters.description: You enter a dense debris field  -- not natural asteroid scatter, but the remains of ships. Multiple ships. Hull fragments from at least three different vessels, all relatively recent. The damage patterns are consistent: weapons fire, concentrated on engine sections. Someone hunted here.

choices.label: Pick Through the Wreckage
choices.description: Three ships, weapons fire, no survivors. Whatever happened here left cargo behind. And maybe answers.

outcome.description: No survivors. You find cargo containers stamped with Commerce Guild seals  -- all empty, contents transferred to whoever did this. One hull fragment still has a name visible: Patience of Kaur. A registered Guild freighter. The attack was recent enough that the metal is still warm in places. You take what you can and leave fast.

choices.label: Scan and Leave Quickly
choices.description: Whoever did this might still be nearby. Get the data and go.

outcome.description: You run a fast sweep  -- three ship IDs, weapon signature profiles, debris age estimates. Enough for a report, if you trust anyone enough to file one. You don't linger. Smart pilots learn from other pilots' mistakes.

choices.label: Plot a Wide Detour
choices.description: Debris fields that used to be ships are a warning. Whoever did this might still be hunting.

outcome.description: You plot a wide detour around the field. It costs time and fuel, but the alternative is joining the wreckage. Some savings aren't worth the risk.

encounters.name: Rogue AI Vessel
encounters.description: An unmarked ship hails you on every frequency simultaneously. The message is a string of equations you cannot parse. Then it resolves into words: "ANALYZING. ENGAGING. PROBABILITY OF YOUR SURVIVAL: DECLINING."

choices.label: Defend Yourself
choices.description: If it wants a fight, it picked the wrong pilot.

outcome.description: The AI ship accelerates to attack speed. Its hull shimmers with adaptive plating.

choices.label: Break Contact
choices.description: An AI that talks to itself is not something you want to fight.

outcome.description: You cut comms and burn hard. The AI vessel does not pursue. Its final transmission: "NOTED. NEXT TIME."

encounters.name: The Corsair King
encounters.description: A warship the size of a station drops out of hyperspace, trailing wreckage and the ghosts of a hundred conquered vessels. The Corsair King's flagship. Its broadside batteries are already turning toward you.

choices.label: Stand and Fight
choices.description: The King has terrorized these lanes long enough. If you can take that ship, you take the crown.

outcome.description: The Corsair King opens with a broadside that rocks your ship to its bolts. This is the fight of your life.

choices.label: Full Burn Away
choices.description: Fighting that thing is suicide. You can outrun it. Probably.

outcome.description: You punch the engines and break away. The King does not pursue. You were beneath notice.

encounters.name: The Iron Maw
encounters.description: The asteroid field ahead is moving. Something IN the field is moving. A hulk of industrial metal and jury-rigged armor, larger than a frigate. The Iron Maw. They said it was a myth. A foundry that learned to hate.

choices.label: Engage the Iron Maw
choices.description: That thing has swallowed ships whole. But whatever powers it must be extraordinary.

outcome.description: The Iron Maw opens its jaws. The heat hits your shields before the weapons do.

choices.label: Reverse Course
choices.description: No cargo is worth dying for. Back away slowly.

outcome.description: You cut engines and drift backward. The Iron Maw does not follow. It has other prey.

encounters.name: The Ledger Phantom
encounters.description: Your sensors are glitching. The readout shows a ship, then nothing, then a ship again in a different position. Your targeting computer gives up and displays a question mark. The Ledger Phantom. A data ghost haunting the lanes since before anyone alive can remember.

choices.label: Lock On and Fire
choices.description: If you can hit something that is barely real, you deserve whatever it drops.

outcome.description: The Phantom flickers. For one moment it is solid, and in that moment it fires.

choices.label: Cut Sensors and Run
choices.description: If you cannot see it, it cannot see you. Maybe.

outcome.description: You kill active sensors and drift away on momentum. The Phantom flickers once more and vanishes.

encounters.name: The Void Leviathan
encounters.description: The stars go dark. Not dimmed. Dark. Something immense is blocking them. Mass readings off the scale. Energy signatures that predate human spaceflight. The Void Leviathan. It does not hunt. It simply consumes whatever it touches.

choices.label: Attack the Leviathan
choices.description: Generations of pilots have run from this thing. You are not going to be one of them.

outcome.description: The Leviathan turns toward you. Your ship feels very, very small.

choices.label: Emergency Jump
choices.description: You cannot fight a force of nature. Get out. Now.

outcome.description: You blind-jump to anywhere that is not here. Your hull groans as the gravity well pulls, but you break free. Barely.

encounters.name: The Collector
encounters.description: A ship that should not exist. Welded together from the hulls of a hundred captured vessels, each one a trophy. Weapon systems from every manufacturer, every era. The Collector does not destroy. It acquires. And it has decided your ship belongs in its collection.

choices.label: Refuse the Collection
choices.description: Your ship is not a trophy. Prove it.

outcome.description: The Collector deploys weapons you have never seen before. This is what happens when one ship has everything.

choices.label: Outrun the Tractor Beams
choices.description: That thing is slow. You can make it out before the beams lock.

outcome.description: You boost away, tractor beams clawing at your hull. You escape. This time.

encounters.name: Unauthorized Passenger
encounters.description: A routine cargo check turns up something the manifest didn't account for: a teenager wedged between crate thirty-nine and forty, half-starved, wearing clothes three sizes too big. They flinch when you open the panel. They can't produce an ID. The story changes every time they tell it. The only consistent details are the bruise patterns on their arms and the way they keep watching the airlock like it might open on its own.

choices.label: Report to Station Security
choices.description: File the stowaway report at the next port. There's a small bounty for unauthorized passengers.

outcome.description: You file the report at the next station. Security takes the kid into processing without a second glance. The bounty hits your account before they're through the door. You try not to think about where processing leads for someone with no ID and no story that holds together.

choices.label: Divert to Haven's Rest
choices.description: The refugee center at Haven's Rest takes in people with nowhere else to go. It's a detour, but they won't ask questions.

outcome.description: Haven's Rest takes the kid in without paperwork or judgment. The intake coordinator gives you a look that's half gratitude, half exhaustion. On the way out, the kid stops at the door and says something too quiet to hear. The coordinator translates: 'They said thank you.' You pretend the detour was on your route anyway.

choices.label: Put Them to Work
choices.description: An extra pair of hands in the cargo hold. They eat less than they're worth, and they clearly know their way around a shipping crate.

outcome.description: The kid works without complaint and without rest. Within a day they've reorganized the hold by weight distribution, something your loading algorithm missed. They don't talk about where they came from. You don't ask. It's a temporary arrangement, but temporary has a way of stretching out here.

encounters.name: Escape Pod Adrift
encounters.description: A faint life sign registers on your sensors. An escape pod tumbles in a slow spin. The beacon is dead and the transponder has been gutted. The hull is scorched along one side, the viewport dark. Whoever is inside stopped trying to signal a long time ago. You can match velocity and pull them aboard, or log the coordinates and move on.

choices.label: Match Velocity and Dock
choices.description: Burn fuel to match the pod's tumble and bring the survivor aboard. It'll cost time and a docking clamp you might need later.

outcome.description: The docking clamp locks onto the pod with a shudder that travels through your whole hull. Inside, a woman in a torn flight suit is barely conscious. Her first words are coordinates, repeated like a prayer. She's been drifting for three days. You set course for the nearest station.

choices.label: Salvage the Pod
choices.description: The pod's hull plating has salvage value. Check for a survivor first, then strip what you can.

outcome.description: You crack the pod's hatch. The survivor inside is dehydrated and incoherent but alive. You pull her out, strip the pod's intact hull plating and fuel cell, and set course for the nearest station. She'll recover. The pod won't.

choices.label: Log Coordinates and Move On
choices.description: Someone closer might reach them. Or someone with more time. You flag the position for the next patrol.

outcome.description: You log the coordinates and transmit them on the emergency frequency. The pod shrinks in your rear sensors until it's just another point of light. The patrol frequency doesn't acknowledge.
